name: Absinthe
whatItIs: Absinthe is used in bar service as part of the liqueurs and amari category.
flavorProfile: Sweetened concentrated flavor; may add fruit, herbs, spice, bitterness, or texture.
typicalFunction: Sweetened concentrated flavor; may add fruit, herbs, spice, bitterness, or texture.
commonSubstitutions: Substitute only when the guest and recipe allow it; re-balance sweetness, acidity, proof, and aroma rather than swapping blindly.
storage: Store tightly closed away from heat and direct light; refrigerate low-proof products when the producer directs.
shelfLifeAfterOpening: Often months to years, but proof, sugar, dairy, and fresh ingredients change the answer.
deteriorationSigns: Discard or replace when aroma fades, flavor becomes stale or oxidized, carbonation is lost, color changes unexpectedly, mold appears, or the product violates its labeled date or workplace policy.
utahLegalClassification: Potential secondary alcoholic flavoring only when lawfully designated and handled
sourceNote: Operational storage guidance is general training information; follow the producer label and workplace food-safety policy.

commonSubstitutions: Substitute only when the guest and recipe allow it; re-balance sweetness, acidity, proof, and aroma rather than swapping blindly.
storage: Refrigerate house-made syrups in sanitized dated containers.

deteriorationSigns: Discard or replace when aroma fades, flavor becomes stale or oxidized, carbonation is lost, color changes unexpectedly, mold appears, or the product violates its labeled date or workplace policy.

whatItIs: Scotch whisky is used in bar service as part of the base spirits category.
flavorProfile: Alcoholic structure with category-specific grain, cane, agave, botanical, or fruit character.
typicalFunction: Alcoholic structure with category-specific grain, cane, agave, botanical, or fruit character.
commonSubstitutions: Substitute only when the guest and recipe allow it; re-balance sweetness, acidity, proof, and aroma rather than swapping blindly.
storage: Store sealed at stable room temperature away from heat and direct light.
shelfLifeAfterOpening: Long shelf life; quality declines mainly through evaporation, contamination, or prolonged oxygen exposure.
deteriorationSigns: Discard or replace when aroma fades, flavor becomes stale or oxidized, carbonation is lost, color changes unexpectedly, mold appears, or the product violates its labeled date or workplace policy.

name: Sparkling wine
whatItIs: Sparkling wine is used in bar service as part of the wines category.
flavorProfile: Fermented fruit character, acidity, aroma, and—when sparkling—carbonation.
typicalFunction: Fermented fruit character, acidity, aroma, and—when sparkling—carbonation.
commonSubstitutions: Substitute only when the guest and recipe allow it; re-balance sweetness, acidity, proof, and aroma rather than swapping blindly.
storage: Refrigerate after opening and use a wine-preservation system when available.
shelfLifeAfterOpening: Sparkling wine is best the same day; still wine usually within several days.
deteriorationSigns: Discard or replace when aroma fades, flavor becomes stale or oxidized, carbonation is lost, color changes unexpectedly, mold appears, or the product violates its labeled date or workplace policy.

name: Sweet vermouth
whatItIs: Sweet vermouth is used in bar service as part of the vermouths category.
flavorProfile: Wine-based, herbal, lightly oxidizable, and lower proof than distilled spirits.
typicalFunction: Wine-based, herbal, lightly oxidizable, and lower proof than distilled spirits.
commonSubstitutions: Substitute only when the guest and recipe allow it; re-balance sweetness, acidity, proof, and aroma rather than swapping blindly.
storage: Refrigerate immediately after opening and minimize oxygen exposure.
shelfLifeAfterOpening: Best within about 4–8 weeks for training-quality service; track the open date.
deteriorationSigns: Discard or replace when aroma fades, flavor becomes stale or oxidized, carbonation is lost, color changes unexpectedly, mold appears, or the product violates its labeled date or workplace policy.
utahLegalClassification: Fortified or aromatized wine; verify product and license treatment
